title: Ultra-super solar absorber element technologies
abstract: The invention relates to an ultra-super solar absorber element (1) for wall (4) and/or roof (13) surfaces, which can be used to obtain energy from sunlight, comprising an outer panel (2) which is transparent at least in sections thereof and has a light-focusing and/or light deflecting structure (3), an inner panel (7), and a core (5), wherein the core (5) comprises a hollow structure, in particular light-transmissive tubes (6) extending from the outer panel (2) to the inner panel (7), in particular honeycomb tubes (6).
claims:
Claims Claims : 1. An ultra-super solar absorber element for wall and/or roof surfaces, comprising an outer panel which is transparent at least in sections thereof and has a light-focusing and/or light-deflecting structure; and a core, wherein the core comprises a hollow structure. 2. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in the preceding claim, further comprising an inner panel, in particular wherein light-transmissive tubes are provided extending from the light-focusing and/or light-deflecting outer panel to the inner panel, in particular honeycomb tubes. 3. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the outer panel is connected to a further panel, in particular a heat exchanger, said further panel in turn being connected to the core. 4. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein the outer panel is connected to the further panel through at least one spacer, the spacing being in particular selected such that light-collecting structures of the outer panel are focused onto the surface of the further panel. 5. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the core is in 

 the form of a honeycomb panel and/or a capillary panel, in particular comprising tubes made of metal, in particular of copper or stainless steel. 6. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the light- focusing and/or light-deflecting structure comprises an array of prisms, mirrors, and/or lenses, in particular of triplet prisms or catadioptric/triplet mirrors, which in particular converts the light into highly efficient, useful, environmentally friendly, cost-effective, almost maintenance-free heat. 7. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein the prisms, mirrors, or lenses are arranged so as to be tilted relative to the panel plane of the outer panel. 8. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the light- transmissive tubes are mirrored. 9. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the ultra-super solar absorber element is mirrored at its edges, in particular by a circumferential metal strip. 10. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the outer panel is made of glass or acrylic. 11. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the outer panel has a hydrophobic surface. 12. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the ultra-super solar absorber element comprises at least one metal tube mat, in particular a metal tube mat made of copper or stainless steel. 13. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the ultra-super solar absorber element comprises a heat exchanger. 14. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein the heat exchanger consists of an aluminum panel having a recess as a fluid guideway, said panel being joined to a preferably flat further aluminum panel, in particular glued or screwed thereto. 15. A heat exchanger for an ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the two preceding claims. 16. An ultra-super solar absorber element, comprising a heat exchanger as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein the heat exchanger is connected, through spacers, to an outer panel that has a light-collecting structure . 17. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein on the side opposite to the outer panel the heat exchanger is connected to a core comprising tubes, in particular honeycombs. 18. The ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein the ultra-super solar absorber element and/or the light-focusing and/or light-deflecting structure are configured so as to be tiltable . 19. A wall or roof surface, comprising an ultra-super solar absorber element as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the wall or roof surface or the inner panel is dark colored, especially black. 20. A building, comprising a wall or roof surface as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein fluid conduits are provided below the ultra-super solar absorber element, which are connected to a thermal energy storage . 21. A system for heat generation, in particular for air conditioning of buildings, in particular using an ultra-super solar absorber element and/or a building according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the system comprises a solar absorber, in particular an ultra-super solar absorber element according to any one of the preceding claims, which is connected to light-conducting fibers through which the light is fed 

 into a heat storage in which it is absorbed and converted into heat. 22. The system as claimed in the preceding claim, wherein light can be injected into the light conducting fibers and/or emitted from the light-conducting fibers via lenses . 23. The system as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the light-conducting fibers are in the form of fiber optic cables. 24. The system as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the heat storage is in the form of a former mrne .
